commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=057b7ca71d2659135ff2344310b8c3265ce47f94)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a native Telegram for macOS Postbox importer with auto-detection, doctor/metadata/docs updates, Docker/dependency changes, and parser fixtures/tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows `deps install` now unconditionally installs SQLCipher bindings, and Postbox raw `--chat` filtering can return account-scoped rows that the CLI still upserts under the raw chat key.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the PR has a prior stop signal and the remaining blockers include a new backend dependency policy plus two pre-merge correctness repairs.

target.title: [codex] import Telegram macOS Postbox archives
target.body:
# Human written summary

The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> Telegram is stupid and has two different versions on MacOS for reasons. Presumably the original version of Telecrawl was written against the other version of Telegram, whichever that one is, which has a different backing database type, it seems. (App Store vs installer, and they have totally different codebases). Anyway, this ensures telecrawl can import from both database types, because I had the other one installed.

# Changes

- Adds an offline native Telegram for macOS Postbox importer for `.tempkeyEncrypted` plus `account-*/postbox/db/db_sqlite`.
- Keeps one import path: `telecrawl import` auto-detects Telegram Desktop `tdata` first, then the native macOS group container; explicit `--path` values are classified automatically.
- Keeps one dependency setup path: `telecrawl deps install` installs bridge dependencies for both supported local Telegram backends.
- Reports Postbox key/database counts in `doctor` and advertises the `telegram-macos-postbox` local-only scope in crawlkit metadata.
- Adds public sanitized Postbox format fixtures under `internal/telegramdesktop/testdata/postbox` plus parser/routing tests, so review does not depend only on private Telegram rows.

# Tests

- `python3 -B internal/telegramdesktop/scripts/import_postbox.py --self-test --fixture-dir internal/telegramdesktop/testdata/postbox` passed.
- `python3 -B -m py_compile internal/telegramdesktop/scripts/import_postbox.py internal/telegramdesktop/scripts/import_tdata.py` passed.
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c go test -count=1 ./...` passed.
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c go test -count=1 -race ./...` passed.
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 nixpkgs#golangci-lint -c golangci-lint run` passed.
- `staticcheck ./...` passed under Nix Go.
- Source-built import from a copied Keepcoder dataset passed: `48,980` messages, `358` chats, `9,919` media-tagged messages, oldest `2015-01-25T11:29:57Z`, newest `2026-05-28T16:31:11Z`.
- Raw decrypted Postbox `t7` key sanity check matched the import count: `48,980` unique local messages.

# Risks

- Postbox parsing is local-cache best effort. It imports text, peer names where locally decodable, timestamps, direction, sender IDs, and media tag categories, but not full Telegram cloud metadata parity with the Telethon `tdata` path.
- SQLCipher availability matters for Postbox imports; Docker and `deps install` include the needed Python/native dependencies.

# Follow-ups

- Extend Postbox media cache extraction beyond media tag categories.
- Add a fuller public SQLCipher/Postbox corpus if a non-private sample can be produced.
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: joshp123
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
